question: q04
question_title: 用数据驱动分析说服客户
statement: Using a data-driven analysis, how will you convince your client that this is a project they should undertake?
methods: 比较无项目贸易额增长路径与 5 年项目干预路径，输出第 5 年降低比例和年度影响表。

parameters.source_note: Official PDF statement parameters only; project rows are deterministic scenario assumptions, not observed enforcement data.

impact_projection.method: compound no-project growth path compared with deterministic yearly intervention reductions under implementation maturity

project_design.project_name: Targeted Corridor Disruption and Demand-Platform Friction Program

interventions.intervention: risk-scored customs inspections on high-probability routes
interventions.client_power_required: customs targeting authority and shipment metadata access
interventions.evidence_basis: network interdiction and targeted inspection are more efficient than uniform screening

interventions.client_power_required: data-sharing MOUs, secure analytics platform, common species/product taxonomy
interventions.evidence_basis: multi-agency intelligence fusion reduces repeated blind spots across borders

interventions.intervention: online listing takedown and seller re-entry friction
interventions.client_power_required: platform APIs, trust-and-safety escalation, payment account flags
interventions.evidence_basis: demand-side transaction friction reduces open-market availability

interventions.intervention: community informant rewards and alternative-livelihood microgrants
interventions.client_power_required: local NGO partners, audited reward fund, community project pipeline
interventions.evidence_basis: source-area incentives reduce recruitment into poaching and improve reporting

interventions.client_power_required: FIU liaison, suspicious transaction typologies, privacy-preserving watchlists
interventions.evidence_basis: following payment channels complements physical seizure data

project_design.suitability_for_client: The selected client can coordinate customs risk scoring, data-sharing rules, and enforcement operations while partnering with platforms, FIUs, and community groups.

client_selection.model: multi-criteria client fit score over mandate, cross-border power, data access, implementation capacity, mission alignment, and political feasibility
client_selection.selected_client: World Customs Organization coordinated customs task force
client_selection.selection_reason: highest fit because customs coordination has authority over border chokepoints and access to shipment-risk metadata

candidate_clients.client: World Customs Organization coordinated customs task force
National park agency in a source country
